title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The way we commute is undergoing a (0) REVOLUTIONARY change thanks to technology. City planners are increasingly (17) about the impact of traffic congestion on the environment. One (18) is the introduction of electric scooters and bikes. These (19) aim to reduce our reliance on private cars. Furthermore, the (20) of existing subway lines is seen as (21) for urban growth. Many citizens find that public transport is more (22) than paying for parking. However, the (23) of these services must be improved to attract more users. With (24) management, cities can become cleaner and more navigable for everyone. By prioritizing green alternatives, we can ensure that future generations enjoy a healthier atmosphere and more efficient ways to travel through their bustling metropolises. This shift requires both political will and public cooperation to succeed in the long term.

example.text: The way we commute is undergoing a (0) REVOLUTIONARY (REVOLUTION) change...
example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'change'; REVOLUTION → REVOLUTIONARY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required after the verb 'are'; CONCERN → CONCERNED.
Noun required as the subject; SOLVE → SOLUTION.
Noun required after the definite article; EXTEND → EXTENSION.
Adjective required to describe public transport; ECONOMY → ECONOMICAL.
Adjective required to modify 'management'; EFFECT → EFFECTIVE.
